gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/263)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/263)
text: أُتِيَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) بِامْرَأَةٍ بِكْرٍ زَعَمُوا أَنَّهَا زَنَتْ فَأَمَرَ النِّسَاءَ فَنَظَرْنَ إِلَيْهَا فَقُلْنَ هِيَ عَذْرَاءُ فَقَالَ مَا كُنْتُ لِأَضْرِبَ مَنْ عَلَيْهَا خَاتَمٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ كَانَ يُجِيزُ ( عليه السلام ) شَهَادَةَ النِّسَاءِ فِي مِثْلِ هَذَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘They came to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> with an unmarried woman alleging that she had committed adultery. So he<sup>asws</sup> ordered the women to look at her, so they said, ‘She is a virgin’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> would not be the one to whip one upon whom is a seal from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic’. And he<sup>asws</sup> used to allow the testimony of the women in (cases) similar to this’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan Shammun from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Abd al- Rahman from Misma‘ ibn ‘ Abd al-Malik who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, Alayhi al-Salam, in the case of an atheist would issue a judgment. If two just and acceptable people testified against him and one thousand in his favor, he (the Imam) would accept the testimony of two people. He (the Imam) would reject the testimony of one thousand because it is a hidden religion.” (It is considered applicable when the testimonies are presented in different times.)
